etymology.description: semantic 己 (己) (jǐ, "oneself") + phonetic 共 (共) (gòng, "together") -> alley; lane
etymology.interpretation: The character 巷 illustrates a concept of joining or confluence, similar to intersecting roads creating an alley or lane. The semantic component 己 (jǐ, "oneself") initially suggests a personal, inner path, which when combined with the phonetic component 共 (gòng, "together"), lends the idea of a shared or common path used by all, such as an alley or lane.

examples.chinese: 我们在这条巷子里迷路了(我們在這條巷子裡迷路了)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen zài zhè tiáo xiàngzi lǐ mílù le.
examples.english: We got lost in this alley.

examples.chinese: 老房子坐落在安静的巷中(老房子坐落在安靜的巷中)。
examples.pinyin: Lǎo fángzi zuòluò zài ānjìng de xiàng zhōng.
examples.english: The old house is located in a quiet alley.

examples.chinese: 小猫常常在巷尾的角落玩耍(小貓常常在巷尾的角落玩耍)。
examples.pinyin: Xiǎo māo chángcháng zài xiàng wěi de jiǎoluò wánshuǎ.
examples.english: The kitten often plays in the corner at the end of the alley.
